Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
HARRY T. GOWER III FROM THE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S OFFICE, 703-1336, appearing for DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY
JOHN M. DRATH FROM DRATH CLIFFORD MURPHY & HAGEN LLP, 510-287-4000, appearing for PLAINTIFF AND RESPONDING PARTY

THE COURT MADE PERSONAL DISCLOSURES, AND NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED AND The Court adopted it's tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL's Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. A jury could find that 2 separate dispatchers entering untrue statements that an ambulance was on it's way constituted an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of care and constituted gross negligence. There is a triable issue material fact whether the resulting delay caused the death. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied. Plaintiff observed effect of the negligence and understood it's consequences. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/jf) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Lisa Schwam, CSR #8994
